title: The Parting Of The Columns
content:
آ آ آ آ Weï؟½ve rode and fought and ate and drunk as rations* come to hand,
آ آ آ آ Together for a year and more around this stinkinï؟½ land:
آ آ آ آ Now you are goinï؟½ home again, but we must see it through.
آ آ آ آ We neednï؟½t tell we liked you well. Good-bye, good luck to you!

آ آ آ آ You ï؟½ad no special call to come, and so you doubled out,
آ آ آ آ And learned us how to camp and cook anï؟½ steal a horse and scout.
آ آ آ آ Whatever game we fancied most, you joyful played it too,
آ آ آ آ And rather better on the whole. Good-bye, good luck to you!

آ آ آ آ There isnï؟½t much we ï؟½ave nï؟½t shared, since Kruger cut and run,
آ آ آ آ The same old work the same old skoff the same old dust and sun;
آ آ آ آ The same old chance that laid us out, or winked anï؟½ let us through;
آ آ آ آ The same old Life, the same old Death. Good-bye, good luck to you!

آ آ آ آ Our blood ï؟½as truly mixed with yours, all down the Red Cross train.
آ آ آ آ Weï؟½ve bit the same thermometer in Bloeming-typhoidtein.**
آ آ آ آ Weï؟½ve ï؟½ad the same old tempï؟½rature, the same relapses too,
آ آ آ آ The same old saw-backed fever-chart. Good-bye, good luck to you!

آ آ آ آ But ï؟½twasnï؟½t merely this anï؟½ that (which all the world may know),
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Twas how you talked anï؟½ looked at things which made us like you so.
آ آ آ آ All independent, queer anï؟½ odd, but most amazinï؟½ new,
آ آ آ آ My word! you shook us up to rights. Good-bye, good luck to you!

آ آ آ آ Think oï؟½ the stories round the fire, the tales along the trek,
آ آ آ آ Oï؟½ Calgary anï؟½ Wellinï؟½ton, anï؟½ Sydney and Quebec;
آ آ آ آ Of mine anï؟½ farm, anï؟½ ranch anï؟½ run, anï؟½ moose anï؟½ cariboo,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ parrots peckinï؟½ lambs to death! Good-bye, good luck to you!

آ آ آ آ Weï؟½ve seen your ï؟½ome by word oï؟½ mouth, weï؟½ve watched your rivers shine,
آ آ آ آ Weï؟½ve ï؟½eard your bloominï؟½ forests blow of eucalipï؟½ and pine;
آ آ آ آ Your young, gay countries north and south, we feel we own ï؟½em too,
آ آ آ آ For they was made by rank anï؟½ file. Good-bye, good luck to you,

آ آ آ آ Weï؟½ll never read the papers now without inquirinï؟½ first
آ آ آ آ For word from all those friendly dorps where you was born anï؟½ nursed.
آ آ آ آ Why, Dawson, Galle, anï؟½ Montreal, Port Darwin, Timaru,
آ آ آ آ Theyï؟½re only just across the road! Good-bye, good luck to you!

آ آ آ آ Good-bye!, So, long! Donï؟½t lose yourselves, nor us, nor all kind friends,
آ آ آ آ But tell the girls your side the drift weï؟½re cominï؟½, when it ends!
آ آ آ آ Good-bye, you bloominï؟½ Atlases! Youï؟½ve taught us somethinï؟½ new:
آ آ آ آ The worldï؟½s no bigger than a kraal. Good-bye, good luck to you!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Rudyard Kipling represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Rudyard Kipling's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
